pages.text: The $(item)Hamlet Skyseed$() — planks, cobblestone and an $(item)emerald$() (mine a Rocky mountain for emeralds). A villager's cottage — the start of trading.
Everything's in hand — here's how it goes together.

A small green on a grassy isle with one or two cottages — the Trade Post's profession shops in miniature, and the seed of a whole trading economy. The first of the village islands. Each is randomly turned and styled to the biome you grew it over, so no two hamlets look quite alike.

$(bold)A trade in hand$()$(br)Each cottage comes with its own $(item)job-site$() — a $(item)composter$(), $(item)lectern$(), $(item)barrel$() or $(item)fletching table$() — and a bed, so its villager settles straight into that trade, dressed to match the biome. Add more beds and job-sites and the hamlet can grow.

$(bold)A home$()$(br)Each cottage has a bed its villager will claim, so they restock and can breed (add more beds and food). $(br)Raids are switched off on Skyseed worlds — illagers would only path into the void — but $(item)iron golems$() still appear once a village grows large enough.

$(bold)A home gone wrong$()$(br)Rarely a Hamlet grows $(o)abandoned$() instead: a cobwebbed ruin with no bed and a $(o)zombie villager$() for a tenant. Cure them and you have a villager — often with a discount.
